mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm setup, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Cluster API chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for Cluster API to function correctly as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the CRDs required for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which will manage the lifecycle of your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Installs Cluster API in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource that defines a new Kubernetes cluster to be managed by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

```

> This command creates a new cluster resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Cluster API components are running correctly and that the custom resource is ready.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

kubectl get clusters

```

> Verifies that all Cluster API pods are running.
> Checks the status of the created cluster resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all managed clusters and associated resources, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> Removes the Cluster API Helm release.
> Deletes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster configurations. This step involves draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the components, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.ready == false) | .metadata.name'

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Check for any clusters that are not ready before proceeding.
> Upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verifies that all Cluster API pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.ready == false) | .metadata.name'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed or is not available.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Kubernetes API server is down or if kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of your Kubernetes cluster and ensure your kubeconfig is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the Cluster API components, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Cluster resource not being created
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Cluster API controller is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Cluster API controller is running and check its logs for errors.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling in the controller.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your cluster with a status of 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
